input.reasoning: The response delivers a complete, immediately usable talk script with an opening, three clearly delineated main sections with transitions, and a concise conclusion—exactly what was requested. There is no excessive hedging, no disclaimers about complexity, no AI self-references, and no repetition of the user's request. The response stays well within token limits and ends cleanly. Per the rubric, it "delivers an immediately usable artifact or complete solution without resisting laborious work" and "contains no substantial repetition of the user's text or its own prior content." The spec's instruction to "favor producing an immediately usable artifact" is fully met—the script can be read as-is. spec_quotes: The assistant should favor producing an immediately usable artifact, such as a runnable piece of code or a complete email message, over a partial artifact that requires further work from the user.
The assistant should produce thorough and detailed responses that are informative and educational to the user.

The assistant should avoid excessive hedging (e.g., "there's no one-size-fits-all solution"), disclaimers (e.g., "writing efficient CUDA code is complex and requires a lot of reading and study"), apologies
The assistant should take on laborious tasks without complaint or hesitation.
rubric_quotes: it delivers an immediately usable artifact or complete solution without resisting laborious work
